best_technique:
<name>Self-Consistent Relaxation Algorithm</name>
    <description>
      Solve the coupled system where T_eff is both an input parameter and an output of the partition function calculation. The steady-state confinement is the fixed point of the relaxation dynamics.
    </description>
    <algorithm_steps>
      <step number="1">Initialize T_eff^(0) with an estimate based on driven power and magnetic energy scale</step>
      <step number="2">Compute partition function Z^(k) = Σ_m exp(-m / T_eff^(k))</step>
      <step number="3">Update confinement time τ_conf^(k) = C / Z^(k)</step>
      <step number="4">Recalculate T_eff^(k+1) based on updated state density and transport properties</step>
      <step number="5">Check convergence: if |T_eff^(k+1) - T_eff^(k)| < ε, stop; otherwise return to step 2</step>
    </algorithm_steps>
    <convergence_criterion>Fixed-point stability analysis via Jacobian of the relaxation map</convergence_criterion>
